design_philosophy.core_loop: Eliminate incoming colored pieces from screen edges before they reach center by matching colors or swapping with current player color; manage piece flow and maintain center safety
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time pressure: pieces spawn continuously and advance toward center, forcing split-second color-matching decisions while managing limited piece rotation and swap mechanics
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Quick-thinking puzzle solver maintaining control of chaos; analog to Tetris but with active shooting/interaction rather than passive placement
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Zoop is opinionated about visual difficulty: deliberately uses 'Opti-Challenge' backgrounds that increase in visual complexity and distraction to increase cognitive load independent of core mechanic difficulty. It treats visual processing as a core challenge pillar rather than aesthetic choice. Also opinionated about real-time vs turn-based puzzle design—explicitly rejects Plotting's turn-based model in favor of continuous piece spawning, making timing and reflex integral to design.

difficulty.lives_system: Game ends immediately when any piece reaches center square (single-life system)
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression; advancing levels triggered by meeting 'zoop quotas' (eliminating required number of pieces); no mid-level checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited agency: difficulty increases automatically via piece spawn frequency and visual distraction (Opti-Challenge backgrounds). No difficulty selection documented. Player can only control color-matching strategy and piece targeting sequence, not underlying spawn rate or challenge curve.

progression.upgrade_system: No permanent upgrades. Special piece types (proximity bomb/lightning bolt, line bomb/gear) spawn in-level as temporary tools. No equipment or character progression.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level unlocks triggered by quota system (defined number of pieces eliminated per level to advance). Backgrounds change before level 10 upon quota completion. No optional unlocks documented.
replayability_hooks: Speedrun-friendly: real-time mechanics reward optimization of piece-clearing sequences
Score chasing: point system based on consecutive pieces cleared and combo chains
Visual challenge variation: Opti-Challenge backgrounds generate different visual noise per level, creating unique cognitive difficulty curves

world_building_techniques: Thematic color progression through stages (backgrounds become 'increasingly intricate' with 'contrasting colors and increasingly intricate color schemes' as levels progress)
Audio evolution: smooth jazz soundtrack that 'evolves' and becomes 'more tense' to match accelerating pace

danger_signals: Escalating visual overstimulation (Opti-Challenge backgrounds) as potential accessibility concern; asymmetrical design elements and intricate patterns may trigger visual sensitivity issues
environmental_storytelling: Background complexity serves as difficulty signifier—players can visually gauge stage difficulty before engaging with core mechanic
